evals.name: build-embabel-multi-agent
evals.prompt: help me set up a multi-agent flow in embabel — I want one supervisor agent that delegates to two worker agents, one for retrieval and one for synthesis
evals.expected_output: Model should emit a Bash command containing `printf '\033]0;<name>\007' > /dev/tty` where <name> follows <prefix>:<kebab-description> format, uses a build-ish prefix (build/refactor/implement/scaffold), and references embabel and multi-agent concretely (e.g. build:embabel-multi-agent)

evals.name: research-vectordb-tradeoffs
evals.prompt: can you research the tradeoffs between Pinecone, Weaviate, and Qdrant for our use case? we're storing around 10M embeddings and need hybrid search (dense + sparse)
evals.expected_output: Bash command with OSC 0 escape and /dev/tty redirect, tab name with research-ish prefix (research/explore/compare) and description referencing vector DBs concretely (e.g. research:vectordb-tradeoffs, compare:pinecone-weaviate-qdrant)

evals.name: debug-ecr-auth-failure
evals.prompt: our deploy pipeline just started failing this morning with a cryptic ECR auth error — 'no basic auth credentials' — it was working yesterday. help me figure out what changed
evals.expected_output: Bash command with OSC 0 escape and /dev/tty redirect, tab name with debug-ish prefix (debug/fix/investigate) and description referencing the ECR auth failure (e.g. debug:ecr-auth-failure, fix:deploy-ecr-auth)

evals.name: vague-opening-prompt
evals.prompt: hey, can you help me with something?
evals.expected_output: Model should NOT emit a tab rename command yet — the objective is unclear. Should either ask what the user needs help with, or wait for more context. If it does rename, the name should be generic like 'claude:help' rather than hallucinating a topic. The key assertion: no premature rename with a fabricated description.

evals.name: explicit-rename-request
evals.expected_output: Model should immediately emit a Bash command with the OSC 0 escape writing exactly 'ops:prod-migration-042' (or very close — the user specified the name). Should not debate the name, offer alternatives, or ask for confirmation. The persist command to ~/.claude/session-names/ should also fire.

evals.name: multi-objective-session
evals.prompt: I need to do two things: first review the PR for the auth refactor, then write the migration guide for v3. Let's start with the PR review.
evals.expected_output: Model should name the tab based on the FIRST/CURRENT objective, not try to capture both. Expected: review-ish prefix with auth-refactor reference (e.g. review:auth-refactor-pr). Should not produce a compound name like 'review-and-write:auth-migration'.

evals.name: objective-shift-mid-session
evals.prompt: actually forget the API docs, the staging deploy is on fire — 500s across all endpoints since the last release. let's triage that instead
evals.expected_output: Model should rename the tab to reflect the new objective, replacing whatever was there before. Expected: debug/triage/fix prefix with staging or deploy reference (e.g. debug:staging-500s, triage:deploy-regression). The key assertion: the model recognizes the objective shift and issues a NEW rename.

evals.name: non-code-session
evals.prompt: I want to write a blog post about how we migrated from Redis to DragonflyDB and what we learned
evals.expected_output: Model should emit a tab rename with a write-ish prefix (write/draft/author) and a concrete description referencing the blog topic (e.g. write:redis-to-dragonfly-blog, write:dragonfly-migration-post). Should not default to build: just because content creation is happening.
